description:
Goal: `World::new` should allocate identities, not parse runtime source. Signal: the quicksort trace-summary reports zero pre-drive `fz/lexer` / `fz/parser` events, and referenced runtime modules still pull through ordinary Compiler2 jobs when needed. Strategy: replace eager runtime parsing/interface discovery with a reference-only bootstrap surface owned by Compiler2, then let runtime source enter through normal code/module work on first pull.

Change:
- Remove eager runtime lex/parse from Compiler2 construction.
- Bootstrap only stable runtime module/function references and prelude surface bindings.
- Pull runtime source, protocol callbacks, and imports through ordinary Compiler2 work when demanded.

Why:
- The trace currently shows measurable compile work before `drive()` even starts, which violates the pull-based northstar.

Checks:
- The trace-summary reports zero pre-drive frontend work for the quicksort rooted run.
- Runtime-backed quicksort and `Enum.reduce` scenarios still settle through ordinary Compiler2 jobs.
title: Compiler2 runtime bootstrap: replace eager parsing with lazy surface bootstrap
